generatedSentence: Elias, once a virtuoso juggler whose repertoire was textbook perfect, now spent his afternoon as a janitor in the old judicial building. He was a deeply antisocial man, constantly pining for a past glory that felt like ancient relics. The weight of his ruined marriage and his own haughty nature had led to his ultimate downfall, leaving his spirit untied and far from jubilant. He'd narrate his failures to himself, polishing the parquet floors until his knuckles ached, a metaphorical wreath of regret upon his brow. He yearned to overcome his current bottom-rung existence, seeking a new vector for his life, a laser focus that would restore the validity of his purpose. He often felt as though he wore an invisible visor, shielding him from the world. Elias, burdened by years of unemployment and a life that felt leaking away, decided to jettison his hesitation. He’d juggled his options, and now approached a mysterious collection of figures in the innkeeper's private office. The leader, a woman with a magnetic gaze and an indigo scarf, had a winsome but unnerving way of watching. She was known for her largesse, but also the abyssal depths of her dealings, often involving strange artifacts, like a legendary kabeljou. Elias, feeling his old clothing was inadequate, still advanced. He pictured a bright future, though he knew many would disagree with his path. He even shared a hilarious anecdote about a broken umbrella to ease the tension. He glimpsed a keyboard in the corner, and imagined a helicopter on the ground outside, ready to transport their stored illicit goods. The only thing drinkable in the room was stale coffee. A single eyelash clung to his cheek, a tiny detail in a monumental decision. The climax of the gathering was a frantic race against time. Elias, now a reluctant coper in their ranks, found himself in a sweltering warehouse where the air felt like it was baking. A handsome trucker, whose voice could bellow like a hippopotamus through an amplifier, warned them to beware. They were attempting a dangerous alchemy, a complex operation with many layers of risk, involving a device that needed to be uplinked within centimeters of its target. Elias, despite slacking off in his past, felt a surge of adrenaline. He knew his lucky break had been a long time coming. The host of this operation, a shadowy figure, promised a premium reward for its successful utilization, a metaphorical vaccine and antibiotic against their troubles. Through the dusty windows, he could vaguely make out the approaching sirens; their time was almost ended. He had been drinking strong coffee all night to stay alert. Despite the operation's success, a murkier feeling remained. Elias couldn't quite swallow the notion that he was now part of something superior. He often found himself replaying the event in his mind, like old replays of a race where jockeys manipulated outcomes. He had seen too much, and the experience had begun to harrow his soul. He yearned for a simpler lifestyle, away from the constant backpedalling required to appease his new, controlling "partners," whose methods felt like a dishonoring of any ethical code. He sometimes thought of them as a destroyer of peace, a criminal enterprise that had hijacked his desperate hope. He sought solace on his small balcony, looking up at the dippers of stars, drinking warm milk from a brewery glass, hoping for some validation. The cold, precise logic of their work, like a temperature measured in kelvin, offered no comfort. He felt like his own future had been taken to a junkyard. His youthful, almost juvenile, desire for quick success had led him to this point, his own voice silent amidst the clamor. It felt like his entire hemisphere of existence had been reshaped.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, dark noir comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Elias, an aging, gaunt man with a tired expression, deep wrinkles around his eyes, and a five-o-clock shadow, wearing a worn-out janitor's uniform. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elias, an aging janitor, polishing a parquet floor, reflecting on past glory, medium shot, low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elias hesitantly approaching a dimly lit office door in an old inn, two shadowy adult figures with sharp features visible inside, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias, sweating and focused, near a large, complex device with blinking lights in a vast, hot warehouse, a burly adult trucker yelling warnings in the background, close-up, dramatic lighting. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias sitting on a small, urban balcony at night, looking up at the stars, holding a glass of milk, a look of weary reflection on his adult face, long shot. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. All human characters depicted are visibly adult, showing mature features.

definitions.definition: Conveying in demeanour the assumption of superiority; disdainful, supercilious.

A large fire, a conflagration.

definitions.definition: Visually dazzling; luminous, lucent, clear, radiant; not dark.
definitions.example: Could you please dim the light? It's far too bright.

definitions.definition: Having a clear, quick intellect; intelligent.
definitions.example: He's very bright. He was able to solve the problem without my help.

definitions.definition: Vivid, colourful, brilliant.
definitions.example: The orange and blue walls of the sitting room were much brighter than the dull grey walls of the kitchen.

definitions.definition: Happy, in good spirits.
definitions.example: I woke up today feeling so bright that I decided to have a little dance.

definitions.definition: Sparkling with wit; lively; vivacious; cheerful.
Illustrious; glorious.
Clear; transparent.
Manifest to the mind, as light is to the eyes; clear; evident; plain.
(chiefly in the plural) That which can be drunk.
Able to be drunk (as liquid).
(of water) Safe to drink.
(of an alcoholic beverage, especially wine) Of good or satisfactory quality.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
The state of having no job; joblessness.
definitions.example: Unemployment made Jack depressed.

definitions.definition: The phenomenon of joblessness in an economy.
definitions.example: Unemployment has been considered a cause of crime.

definitions.definition: The level of joblessness in an economy, often measured as a percentage of the workforce.
definitions.example: Unemployment was reported at 5.2% in May, up from 4.9% in April.

definitions.definition: A type of joblessness due to a particular economic mechanism.
definitions.example: All unemployments, seasonal, frictional, cyclical, classical, whatever, mean that you're out of work.

definitions.definition: An instance or period of joblessness.
definitions.example: Until then his life had consisted of low-paying jobs, numberous unemployments, and drug use.

definitions.definition: (collective) Items that have been or are about to be ejected from a boat or balloon.
The action of jettisoning items.
To eject from a boat, submarine, aircraft, spaceship or hot-air balloon, so as to lighten the load.
definitions.example: The ballooners had to jettison all of their sand bags to make it over the final hill.

definitions.definition: To let go or get rid of as being useless or defective.

To manipulate objects, such as balls, clubs, beanbags, rings, etc. in an artful or artistic manner. Juggling may also include assorted other circus skills such as the diabolo, devil sticks, hat, and cigar box manipulation as well.
definitions.example: She can juggle flaming torches.

definitions.definition: To handle or manage many tasks at once.
definitions.example: He juggled home, school, and work for two years.

definitions.definition: To deceive by trick or artifice.
To joke or jest.
To perform magic tricks.

An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.
definitions.example: We flew over the city in a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A powered troweling machine with spinning blades used to spread concrete.
The winged fruit of certain trees, such as ash, elm, and maple.
A dragonfly.
A whirling trick performed with devil sticks.
To transport by helicopter.
To travel by helicopter.
To rotate like a helicopter blade.
definitions.example: helicoptering his jacket, helicoptering his arms

definitions.definition: To overprotect one's children.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
definitions.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!

definitions.definition: Terrain.
Soil, earth.
definitions.example: The fox escaped from the hounds by going to ground.

definitions.definition: The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(chiefly in the plural) Reason, (epistemic) justification, cause.
definitions.example: He could not come on grounds of health, or on health grounds.

definitions.definition: Background, context, framework, surroundings.
The area on which a battle is fought, particularly as referring to the area occupied by one side or the other. Often, according to the eventualities, "to give ground" or "to gain ground".
(by extension) Advantage given or gained in any contest; e.g. in football, chess, debate or academic discourse.
The plain surface upon which the figures of an artistic composition are set.
definitions.example: crimson flowers on a white ground

definitions.definition: In sculpture, a flat surface upon which figures are raised in relief.
In point lace, the net of small meshes upon which the embroidered pattern is applied.
definitions.example: Brussels ground

definitions.definition: In etching, a gummy substance spread over the surface of a metal to be etched, to prevent the acid from eating except where an opening is made by the needle.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the pieces of wood, flush with the plastering, to which mouldings etc. are attached.
definitions.example: Grounds are usually put up first and the plastering floated flush with them.

definitions.definition: A soccer stadium.
definitions.example: Manchester United's ground is known as Old Trafford.

definitions.definition: An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).

The area of grass on which a match is played (a cricket field); the entire arena in which it is played; the part of the field behind a batsman's popping crease where he can not be run out (hence to make one's ground).
A composition in which the bass, consisting of a few bars of independent notes, is continually repeated to a varying melody.
The tune on which descants are raised; the plain song.
The pit of a theatre.
To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing him/her to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges.
definitions.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To forbid (an aircraft or pilot) to fly.
definitions.example: Because of the bad weather, all flights were grounded.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.
definitions.example: Jim was grounded in maths.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).
To place something on the ground.
To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.
definitions.example: The ship grounded on the bar.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.

definitions.definition: To keep (something) while not in use, generally in a place meant for that purpose.
definitions.example: I'll store these books in the attic.

definitions.definition: To write (something) into memory or registers.
definitions.example: This operation stores the result on the stack.

definitions.definition: (etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: Generosity in the giving of gifts or money.
The gifts or money given in such a way.
A benevolent demeanor.
To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.
definitions.example: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.

definitions.definition: To fail to conform or correspond with.
definitions.example: My results in the laboratory consistently disagree with yours.

definitions.definition: Very funny; causing great merriment and laughter.
definitions.example: a hilarious joke

definitions.definition: Full of hilarity; merry.
Belonging to, or resembling, an abyss; unfathomable.
Of or belonging to the ocean depths, especially below 2000 metres (6500 ft): abyssal zone.
Pertaining to or occurring at excessive depths in the earth's crust; plutonic.
To adorn or cover with clothing; to dress; to supply clothes or clothing.
definitions.example: to feed and clothe a family; to clothe oneself extravagantly

definitions.definition: To cover or invest, as if with a garment.
definitions.example: to clothe somebody with authority or power

definitions.definition: Any of a wide variety of articles, usually made of fabrics, animal hair, animal skin, or some combination thereof, used to cover the human body for warmth, to preserve modesty, or for fashion.
An act or instance of putting clothes on.
definitions.example: The clothing and unclothing of the idols was of special significance.

definitions.definition: The art or process of making cloth.
A covering of non-conducting material on the outside of a boiler, or steam chamber, to prevent radiation of heat.
A prize or award.
Something offered at a reduced price as an inducement to buy something else.
A bonus paid in addition to normal payments.
The amount to be paid for an insurance policy.
An unusually high value.
The amount by which a security's value exceeds its face value.
Superior in quality; higher in price or value.
High-end; belonging to the market segment between mid-market and luxury.
A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: A person who lays things, such as tiles.
A mature female bird, insect, etc. that is able to lay eggs.
definitions.example: When dealing with an infestation of headlice, the first step is to eliminate the layers.

definitions.definition: A hen kept to lay eggs.
A shoot of a plant, laid underground for growth.
The act of using something.
The manner in which something is used.
The state of being used.
One who has done something offensive; a deceitful, dishonest, or disreputable person; a deceiver; a cheat.
A rogue; rascal; miscreant; a ne'er-do-well.
Dishonest; resulting from or inspired by dishonesty.
One who copes.
An SI unit of length equal to 10-2 metres. Symbol: cm
To slacken.
To mitigate; to reduce the strength of.
To lose cohesion or solidity by a chemical combination with water; to slake.
definitions.example: Lime slacks.

definitions.definition: (of people) Favoured by luck; fortunate; having good success or good fortune
definitions.example: The downed pilot is very lucky to be alive.

definitions.definition: Producing, or resulting in, good fortune
definitions.example: a lucky cast

definitions.definition: The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant.
definitions.example: To separate out the chaff, early cultures tossed baskets of grain into the air and let the wind blow away the lighter chaff.

definitions.definition: Straw or hay cut up fine for the food of cattle.
Any excess or unwanted material, resource, or person; anything worthless.
Light jesting talk; banter; raillery.
Loose material, e.g. small strips of aluminum foil dropped from aircraft, intended to interfere with radar detection.
An opening, usually covered by one or more panes of clear glass, to allow light and air from outside to enter a building or vehicle.
An opening, usually covered by glass, in a shop which allows people to view the shop and its products from outside; a shop window.
The shutter, casement, sash with its fittings, or other framework, which closes a window opening.
A period of time when something is available.
definitions.example: launch window;  window of opportunity;  You have a two-hour window of clear weather to finish working on the lawn.

definitions.definition: A restricted range.

A rectangular area on a computer terminal or screen containing some kind of user interface, displaying the output of and allowing input for one of a number of simultaneously running computer processes.
A figure formed of lines crossing each other.
The time between first infection and detectability.
To furnish with windows.
To place at or in a window.
To come to an end
definitions.example: Is this movie never going to end?

definitions.definition: To finish, terminate.
definitions.example: The referee blew the whistle to end the game.

definitions.definition: (in combination) Having (a specified kind or number of) ends.
The deep roar of a large animal, or any similar loud noise.
To make a loud, deep, hollow noise like the roar of an angry bull.
To shout in a deep voice.
(with person as subject) To cook (something) in an oven.
definitions.example: I baked a delicious cherry pie.

definitions.definition: (with baked thing as subject) To be cooked in an oven.
definitions.example: The cake baked at 350°F.

definitions.definition: To be warmed to drying and hardening.
definitions.example: The clay baked in the sun.

definitions.definition: To dry by heat.
definitions.example: They baked the electrical parts lightly to remove moisture.

definitions.definition: To be hot.
definitions.example: I'm baking after that workout in the gym.

definitions.definition: To cause to be hot.
To smoke marijuana.
To harden by cold.
To fix (lighting, reflections, etc.) as part of the texture of an object to improve rendering performance.
(with "in" or "into") To incorporate into something greater.
An action in which something is baked.
definitions.example: I'm going to do some baking this afternoon.

definitions.definition: The way in which something is baked.
The production of a batch of baked product.
That bakes.
definitions.example: baking bread   baking clay

definitions.definition: Of a person, an object, or the weather: very hot; boiling, broiling, roasting.
definitions.example: I'm baking – could you open the window?

definitions.definition: One which receives or entertains a guest, socially, commercially, or officially.
definitions.example: A good host is always considerate of the guest’s needs.

definitions.definition: One that provides a facility for an event.
A person or organization responsible for running an event.
definitions.example: Our company is host of the annual conference this year.

definitions.definition: A moderator or master of ceremonies for a performance.
definitions.example: The host was terrible, but the acts themselves were good.

definitions.definition: Any computer attached to a network.
A cell or organism which harbors another organism or biological entity, usually a parasite.
definitions.example: Viruses depend on the host that they infect in order to be able to reproduce.

definitions.definition: (evolution) An organism bearing certain genetic material.
definitions.example: The so-called junk DNA is known, so far, to provide no apparent benefit to its host.

definitions.definition: A paid male companion offering conversation and in some cases sex, as in certain types of bar in Japan.
To perform the role of a host.
definitions.example: I was terrible at hosting that show.

definitions.definition: To lodge at an inn.
To run software made available to a remote user or process.
definitions.example: Kremvax hosts a variety of services.

definitions.definition: A large, semi-aquatic, herbivorous (plant-eating) African mammal (Hippopotamus amphibius)
Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
An adverb that adds intensity, such as "really" or "totally".
An appliance or circuit that increases the strength of a weak electrical signal without changing the other characteristics of the signal.
A portable encasement that houses a large speaker, used to amplify voices and musical instruments at live performances.
A lens that enlarges the field of vision.
Any substance that can destroy or inhibit the growth of bacteria and similar microorganisms, generally transported by the lymphatic system.
(pharmaceutical effect) Of or relating to antibiotics.
Of or relating to the theory that extraterrestrial life does not exist.
A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.
definitions.example: I met her at a gathering of engineers and scientists.

definitions.definition: A group of people or things.
definitions.example: A gathering of fruit.

definitions.definition: A section, a group of bifolios, or sheets of paper, stacked together and folded in half.
definitions.example: This gathering machine forms the backbone of a bookbinding operation.

definitions.definition: A charitable contribution; a collection.
A tumor or boil suppurated or maturated; an abscess.

A substance given to stimulate the body's production of antibodies and provide immunity against a disease without causing the disease itself in the treatment, prepared from the agent that causes the disease (or a related, also effective, but safer disease), or a synthetic substitute.
The ancient search for a universal panacea, and of the philosopher's stone, that eventually developed into chemistry.
The causing of any sort of mysterious sudden transmutation.
Any elaborate transformation process or algorithm.
In a vague manner.
definitions.example: He looked vaguely around the room, as if lost.

definitions.definition: Loosely; more or less; somewhat.
(defective) To use caution, pay attention to (used both with and without of).

A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
An act or session by which drink is consumed, especially alcoholic beverages.
To transmit a signal by means of an uplink.
To render handsome.
(of people, things, etc) Having a good appearance; good-looking.
definitions.example: a handsome garment, house, tree, or horse

definitions.definition: Good, appealing, appropriate.
Generous or noble in character.
definitions.example: Handsome is as handsome does.

definitions.definition: Ample; moderately large.
definitions.example: a handsome salary

definitions.definition: (said of things and people) Dexterous; skillful.
A deep chasm or abyss in the earth.
The amount swallowed in one gulp; the act of swallowing.
definitions.example: He took the aspirin with a single swallow of water.

definitions.definition: Any of various carbohydrate-based dishes that are swallowed without much chewing.
To cause (food, drink etc.) to pass from the mouth into the stomach; to take into the stomach through the throat.
To take (something) in so that it disappears; to consume, absorb.
To take food down into the stomach; to make the muscular contractions of the oesophagus to achieve this, often taken as a sign of nervousness or strong emotion.
definitions.example: My throat was so sore that I was unable to swallow.

definitions.definition: To accept easily or without questions; to believe, accept.
To engross; to appropriate; usually with up.
To retract; to recant.
definitions.example: to swallow one's opinions

definitions.definition: To put up with; to bear patiently or without retaliation.
definitions.example: to swallow an affront or insult

definitions.definition: (stative) To perceive or detect with the eyes, or as if by sight.
To form a mental picture of.
(social) To meet, to visit.
To be the setting or time of.
definitions.example: 1999 saw the release of many great films.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To ensure that something happens, especially while witnessing it.
definitions.example: I'll see you hang for this!  I saw that they didn't make any more trouble.

definitions.definition: To wait upon; attend, escort.
definitions.example: I saw the old lady safely across the road.

definitions.definition: To respond to another player's bet with a bet of equal value.
definitions.example: I'll see your twenty dollars and raise you ten.

definitions.definition: To determine by trial or experiment; to find out (if or whether).
definitions.example: I'll come over later and see if I can fix your computer.

definitions.definition: (used in the imperative) To reference or to study for further details.
definitions.example: For a complete proof of the Poincaré conjecture, see Appendix C.

definitions.definition: To examine something closely, or to utilize something, often as a temporary alternative.
definitions.example: Can I see that lighter for a second? Mine just quit working.

definitions.definition: To include as one of something's experiences.
definitions.example: The equipment has not seen usage outside of our projects.

definitions.definition: A person of higher rank or quality.
The senior person in a monastic community.
The head of certain churches and colleges.
One who has made an original grant of heritable property to a tenant or vassal, on condition of a certain annual payment (feu duty) or of the performance of certain services.
Higher in quality.
definitions.example: Rebecca had always thought shorts were far superior to pants, as they didn't constantly make her legs itch.

definitions.definition: Higher in rank.
More comprehensive, as a term in classification.
definitions.example: A genus is superior to a species.

definitions.definition: Located above.
definitions.example: the superior jaw; the superior part of an image

definitions.definition: Greater or better than average; extraordinary.
Beyond the power or influence of; too great or firm to be subdued or affected by; with to.
Of a planet: closer to the Earth than to the sun.
In the International System of Units, the base unit of thermodynamic temperature; 1/273.16 of the thermodynamic temperature of the triple point of water. Shown as "K".
A unit interval on the Kelvin scale.
definitions.example: The interval between the freezing and boiling points of water is 100 kelvins.

definitions.definition: (usually as postpositioned adjective) A unit for a specific temperature on the Kelvin scale.
definitions.example: Ice melts above 273.15 kelvin.

definitions.definition: The act of validating something.
Something, such as a certificate, that validates something; attestation, authentication, confirmation, proof or verification.
The process whereby others confirm the validity of one's emotions.
The process of identifying a new prisoner's gang affiliation.
A building where beer is produced.
A company that brews beer.
To pedal backwards on a bicycle.
To step backwards.
definitions.example: The player had to backpedal before catching the ball.

definitions.definition: To distance oneself from an earlier claim or statement; back off from an idea.
definitions.example: Though initially adopting a hard-line stance, the politician soon started to backpedal.

definitions.definition: Men's or boys' briefs.
A person who makes, repairs, or alters clothes professionally, especially suits and men's clothing.
definitions.example: He works as a tailor on Swanston Street.

definitions.definition: The bluefish (Pomatomus saltatrix).
To make, repair, or alter clothes.
definitions.example: We can tailor that jacket for you if you like.

definitions.definition: To make or adapt (something) for a specific need.
definitions.example: The website was tailored to the client's needs.

definitions.definition: To restrict (something) in order to meet a particular need.
definitions.example: a narrowly tailored law

definitions.definition: The replaying of (something), for example of televised footage.
definitions.example: Show us that replay one more time.

definitions.definition: Saved video footage of the gameplay of a computer game
A replayed match, often after the first game or match ended in a draw.
A style of living that reflects the attitudes and values of a person or group.

The totality of the likes and dislikes of a particular section of the market, especially when expressed in terms of the products and services that they would buy; a marketing strategy based on the self-image of such a group.
Hard to see through, as a fog or mist.
Dark, dim, gloomy.
Cloudy, indistinct, obscure.
Dishonest, shady.

A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of female mammals to nourish their young. From certain animals, especially cows, it is also called dairy milk and is a common food for humans as a beverage or used to produce various dairy products such as butter, cheese, and yogurt.
A white (or whitish) liquid obtained from a vegetable source such as almonds, coconuts, oats, rice, and/or soy beans. Also called non-dairy milk.
An individual serving of milk.
definitions.example: Table three ordered three milks. (Formally: The guests at table three ordered three glasses of milk.)

definitions.definition: An individual portion of milk, such as found in a creamer, for tea and coffee.
definitions.example: I take my tea with two milk(s) and two sugar.

definitions.definition: The ripe, undischarged spat of an oyster.
Semen.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
definitions.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

definitions.definition: Sound made through vibration of the vocal cords; sonant, or intonated, utterance; tone; — distinguished from mere breath sound as heard in whispering and voiceless consonants.
The tone or sound emitted by an object
The faculty or power of utterance
definitions.example: to cultivate the voice

definitions.definition: That which is communicated; message; meaning.
An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
Command; precept.
One who speaks; a speaker.
A particular style or way of writing that expresses a certain tone or feeling.

(grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
definitions.example: The verbal system of Latin has two voices, active and passive.

definitions.definition: In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
definitions.example: The theme of this piece constantly migrates between the three voice parts.

definitions.definition: (IRC) A flag associated with a user on a channel, determining whether or not they can send messages to the channel.
An accessible structure extending from a building, especially outside a window.
An accessible structure overlooking a stage or the like.
One who, or that which, dips (immerses something, or itself, into a liquid).
Any of various small passerine birds of the genus Cinclus that live near fast-flowing streams and feed along the bottom.
A cup-shaped vessel with a long handle, for dipping into and ladling out liquids; a ladle or scoop.
The control in a vehicle that switches between high-beam and low-beam (i.e. dips the lights), especially when used to signal other vehicles.
Any snack food intended to be dipped in sauce.
definitions.example: chicken dippers

definitions.definition: A pickpocket.
A person employed in a tin plate works to coat steel plates in molten tin by dipping them.
A person employed to assist a bather in and out of the sea.
A Baptist or Dunker.
To bring disgrace upon someone or something; to shame.
definitions.example: You have dishonoured the family.

definitions.definition: To refuse to accept something, such as a cheque; to not honor.
To violate or rape.
That which destroys something.
A small, fast warship with light armament, smaller than a cruiser, but bigger than a frigate.
A place where rubbish is placed.
A business that sells used metal or items.
Half of the celestial sphere, as divided by either the ecliptic or the celestial equator .
A realm or domain of activity .
Half of the Earth, such as the Northern Hemisphere, Southern Hemisphere, Western Hemisphere or Eastern Hemisphere, Land Hemisphere, Water Hemisphere etc. .
Any half-sphere, formed by a plane intersecting the center of a sphere. .
A map or projection of a celestial or terrestrial hemisphere .
Either of the two halves of the cerebrum. .
A device consisting of a heavy framework having several disks or teeth in a row, which is dragged across ploughed land to smooth or break up the soil, to remove weeds or cover seeds; a harrow plow.
An obstacle formed by turning an ordinary harrow upside down, the frame being buried.
To drag a harrow over; to break up with a harrow.
To traumatize or disturb; to frighten or torment.
To break or tear, as if with a harrow; to wound; to lacerate; to torment or distress; to vex.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
Of a vehicle, aircraft, vessel, computer, etc.: whose control has been seized by force
